title: Windows Account Lockout Storm
briefing: Account $USER1 on $TARGET fired a wave of failed logons (Event ID 4625) from a single external source and then tripped a lockout (Event ID 4740). Confirm the account, the source, and whether this is a targeted guess or a misconfigured service.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100110 (4625) then rule.id: 100113 (4740 lockout). Pivot on data.win.eventdata.targetUserName and data.srcip.

questions.prompt: Which account was targeted and locked out?
questions.explain: The 4625 failures and the 4740 lockout both name targetUserName = $USER1.

questions.prompt: What is the attacking source IP?
questions.explain: All 4625 events carry ipAddress = $SRC1 (surfaced as data.srcip).
10+ rapid failures from one external IP against one account, ending in lockout = deliberate guessing, not a typo.

options: Confirm the account owner + reset if needed
Permanently disable the account with no review
answer: Confirm the account owner + reset if needed
questions.explain: Block source, validate/rotate the account, sanity-check lockout policy so it actually slows guessing.
